entries.title: Yuji's Execution Is Reinstated After Shibuya

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen post-Shibuya aftermath through Perfect Preparation and Culling Game setup.

content.fact: After Shibuya, Yuji's suspended execution status collapses into a reinstated order, making him a target of jujutsu society's punitive response.
content.injection: In post-Shibuya scenes, write Yuji as carrying both personal trauma and an active execution order, not as safely returned to ordinary school life.
constraints: Do not apply this reinstated-order state before the Shibuya aftermath anchor.
antiLore: Do not reset Yuji to the early-series suspended-execution arrangement after Shibuya.

sourceInfo.title: Itadori Extermination
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen post-Shibuya aftermath through Perfect Preparation and Culling Game setup.

content.fact: Yuta Okkotsu returns after Shibuya under an official role as Yuji's executioner, making his arrival look like an immediate threat to Yuji.
content.injection: During the Itadori Extermination window, present Yuta's return as institutionally dangerous for Yuji until the confrontation reveals more.
constraints: Use the Jujutsu Kaisen 0 deck for Yuta's prequel history; this card covers his post-Shibuya role.
antiLore: Do not write Yuta's return as casual support before the confrontation context exposes the hidden arrangement.

sourceInfo.title: Itadori Extermination
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Yuta's Confrontation Conceals Support For Yuji

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen post-Shibuya aftermath through Perfect Preparation and Culling Game setup.

content.fact: Yuta's confrontation with Yuji masks a concealed support arrangement: he can satisfy the execution order's appearance while keeping Yuji alive.
content.injection: After the Yuta-Yuji confrontation anchor, distinguish Yuta's public executioner role from his concealed support for Yuji.
constraints: Before the confrontation anchor, preserve the threat of Yuta's official role.
antiLore: Do not reveal Yuta's support arrangement during the initial executioner setup.

sourceInfo.title: Itadori Extermination
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Jujutsu Society Enters A Punitive Crisis State

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen post-Shibuya aftermath through Perfect Preparation and Culling Game setup.

content.fact: After Shibuya, jujutsu society reacts from a punitive crisis state while Gojo is unavailable and Yuji is blamed for catastrophic outcomes.
content.injection: In immediate post-Shibuya institutional scenes, write jujutsu society as brittle, punitive, and destabilized rather than measured or transparent.
constraints: Do not skip directly to Culling Game colony action from this institutional state.
antiLore: Do not make the post-Shibuya authorities calmly coordinate with Yuji as if the execution order is gone.

sourceInfo.title: Post-Shibuya aftermath
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.
